message:
perf(combinator,sync): batch metric updates, pre-compute refill rate, and split cache lines for hot-path counters

Bulkhead (src/combinator/bulkhead.rs):
- Batch timeout metric increments: count all timed-out entries locally
  first, then acquire the metrics write lock once for the total instead
  of once per expired entry — eliminates N lock round-trips on timeout
  sweeps where N entries expire simultaneously

CircuitBreaker (src/combinator/circuit_breaker.rs):
- Add FailurePredicate::is_all_errors() helper that returns true for
  the AllErrors variant without inspecting the error string
- Short-circuit record_failure() when is_all_errors() is true to skip
  the string comparison entirely
- In call(): skip the e.to_string() heap allocation for AllErrors
  predicates by passing an empty string — the record_failure path
  never inspects it when is_all_errors() returns true

RateLimiter (src/combinator/rate_limit.rs):
- Pre-compute tokens_per_ms_fixed at construction time and store it
  on the struct, eliminating a floating-point division on every
  refill_inner() call in the hot path
- SlidingWindowRateLimiter::try_acquire: replace separate cleanup_old()
  call (which took its own lock) with inline pop_front() loop under
  the single write lock, and remove the now-redundant time filter
  from the usage summation since all remaining entries are guaranteed
  to be within the window

ContendedMutex (src/sync/contended_mutex.rs):
- Split Metrics struct into two cache lines using #[repr(C)] layout
  and explicit 32-byte padding: lock-path counters (acquisitions,
  contentions, wait_ns, max_wait_ns) on cache line 1, unlock-path
  counters (hold_ns, max_hold_ns) on cache line 2 — prevents false
  sharing between the lock acquisition thread and the guard-drop
  thread which update different counter groups concurrently

Semaphore (src/sync/semaphore.rs):
- Simplify OwnedAcquireFuture::poll borrow fix: extract waiter_id
  into a local before locking instead of cloning the entire Arc,
  avoiding an atomic ref-count increment on every poll

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -52,13 +52,22 @@ mod inner {
     use std::sync::{LockResult, Mutex, MutexGuard, PoisonError};
     use std::time::Instant;
 
+    /// Metrics counters split into two cache lines to avoid false sharing.
+    /// Lock-path counters (acquisitions, contentions, wait_ns, max_wait_ns) are
+    /// updated during lock(); unlock-path counters (hold_ns, max_hold_ns) are
+    /// updated during drop(Guard). Separating them prevents cross-invalidation.
     #[derive(Debug)]
+    #[repr(C)]
     struct Metrics {
+        // ── Cache line 1: updated on lock() ──
         acquisitions: AtomicU64,
         contentions: AtomicU64,
         wait_ns: AtomicU64,
-        hold_ns: AtomicU64,
         max_wait_ns: AtomicU64,
+        // Pad to 64 bytes (4 × 8 = 32 bytes of data, 32 bytes padding)
+        _pad: [u8; 32],
+        // ── Cache line 2: updated on drop(Guard) ──
+        hold_ns: AtomicU64,
         max_hold_ns: AtomicU64,
     }
 
@@ -68,8 +77,9 @@ mod inner {
                 acquisitions: AtomicU64::new(0),
                 contentions: AtomicU64::new(0),
                 wait_ns: AtomicU64::new(0),
-                hold_ns: AtomicU64::new(0),
                 max_wait_ns: AtomicU64::new(0),
+                _pad: [0; 32],
+                hold_ns: AtomicU64::new(0),
                 max_hold_ns: AtomicU64::new(0),
             }
         }
